name: zope-structuredtext

metadata.name: zope.structuredtext
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Zope Public License

documentation:
# zope.structuredtext

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/zope.structuredtext.svg
:alt: Latest release
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/zope.structuredtext/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/zope.structuredtext.svg
:alt: Supported Python versions
:target: https://pypi.org/project/zope.structuredtext/
```

```{image} https://github.com/zopefoundation/zope.structuredtext/actions/workflows/tests.yml/badge.svg
:target: https://github.com/zopefoundation/zope.structuredtext/actions/workflows/tests.yml
```

```{image} https://coveralls.io/repos/github/zopefoundation/zope.structuredtext/badge.svg?branch=master
:target: https://coveralls.io/github/zopefoundation/zope.structuredtext?branch=master
```

```{image} https://readthedocs.org/projects/zopestructuredtext/badge/?version=latest
:alt: Documentation Status
:target: https://zopestructuredtext.readthedocs.org/en/latest/
```

This package provides a parser and renderers for the classic Zope
"structured text" markup dialect (STX). STX is a plain text markup in
which document structure is signalled primarily by indentation

Please see <https://zopestructuredtext.readthedocs.org/> for documentation.

# Changes

## 5.0 (2023-05-09)

- Drop support for Python 2.7, 3.5, 3.6.
- Drop support for deprecated `python setup.py test`.
- Add support for Python 3.11.

## 4.4 (2022-03-18)

- Add support for Python 3.8, 3.9 and 3.10.
- Drop support for Python 3.4.

## 4.3 (2018-10-09)

- Add support for Python 3.7.

## 4.2.0 (2017-09-05)

- Add support for Python 3.5 and 3.6.
- Drop support for Python 2.6 and 3.3.
- Add support for PyPy and PyPy3.
- Support several new elements (inner and named links, underlines,
  etc) in the docbook writer.
- Fix the XML output of `DocBookBook`.
- 100% test coverage, maintained by CI and tox.
- Unused internal code in the `stdom` module was removed. See [issue
  3](https://github.com/zopefoundation/zope.structuredtext/issues/3).

## 4.1.0 (2014-12-29)

- Drop dependency on `six`.
- Add support for Python 3.4.
- Add support for testing on Travis.

## 4.0.0 (2013-02-25)

- Add support for Python 3.3.
- Drop support for Python 2.4 and 2.5.

## 3.5.1 (2010-12-03)

- Remove antique copyright assertions in regression texts, in conformance
  with repository policy.

## 3.5.0 (2010-04-30)

- Update docs to conform to ZTK / Sphinx usage.
- LP #120376: Output valid html for non-ASCII characters.

## 3.4.0 (2007/09/01)

- Public release for completeness of Zope 3.4.

## 3.2.0 (2006/01/05)

- Corresponds to the verison of the `zope.structuredtext` package shipped
  as part of the Zope 3.2.0 release.
- Only coding style / documentation changes.

## 3.0.0 (2004/11/07)

- Corresponds to the verison of the `zope.structuredtext` package shipped
  as part of the Zope X3.0.0 release.

modules.name: zope.structuredtext
documentation:
Zope structured text markeup

Consider the following example:

```
>>> from zope.structuredtext.stng import structurize
>>> from zope.structuredtext.document import DocumentWithImages
>>> from zope.structuredtext.html import HTMLWithImages
>>> from zope.structuredtext.docbook import DocBook
>>> from zope.structuredtext.docbook import DocBookChapterWithFigures
>>> from zope.structuredtext.docbook import DocBookArticle
```

We first need to structurize the string and make a full-blown
document out of it:

```
>>> structured_string = '''
... Title Here
...
...     Body text here.'''
>>> struct = structurize(structured_string)
>>> doc = DocumentWithImages()(struct)
```

Now feed it to some output generator, in this case HTML or DocBook:

```
>>> HTMLWithImages()(doc, level=1)
'<html>...'
>>> DocBook()(doc, level=1)
'<!DOCTYPE book ...<book>...'
>>> DocBookArticle()(doc, level=1)
'<!DOCTYPE article ...<article>...'
>>> DocBookChapterWithFigures()(doc, level=1)
'<chapter>...'
```

For HTML, there is a shortcut:

```
>>> from zope.structuredtext import stx2html
>>> stx2html(structured_string)
'<html>...'
```

If we have references in the text we can use a different function:

```
>>> from zope.structuredtext import stx2htmlWithReferences
>>> stx2htmlWithReferences(structured_string)
'<html>...'
```

methods.name: zope.structuredtext.stdom.ParentNode.getChildNodes
documentation:
Returns a NodeList that contains all children of this node.
If there are no children, this is a empty NodeList

methods.name: zope.structuredtext.stdom.ParentNode.getFirstChild
documentation:
The first child of this node. If there is no such node
this returns None

methods.name: zope.structuredtext.stdom.ParentNode.getLastChild
documentation:
The last child of this node.  If there is no such node
this returns None.

A node that can have children, or, more precisely, that implements
the child access methods of the DOM.
Node classes

methods.name: zope.structuredtext.stdom.NodeWrapper.getParentNode
documentation:
The parent of this node.  All nodes except Document
DocumentFragment and Attr may have a parent

methods.name: zope.structuredtext.stdom.NodeWrapper.getPreviousSibling
documentation:
The node immediately preceding this node.  If
there is no such node, this returns None.

methods.name: zope.structuredtext.stdom.NodeWrapper.getNextSibling
documentation:
The node immediately preceding this node.  If
there is no such node, this returns None.

methods.name: zope.structuredtext.stdom.NodeWrapper.getOwnerDocument
documentation: The Document object associated with this node, if any.

This is an acquisition-like wrapper that provides parent access for
DOM sans circular references!

Retrieves an Attr node by name or None if

methods.name: zope.structuredtext.stdom.Element.getElementsByTagName
documentation:
Returns a NodeList of all the Elements with a given tag
name in the order in which they would be encountered in a
preorder traversal of the Document tree.  Parameter: tagname
The name of the tag to match (* = all tags). Return Value: A new
NodeList object containing all the matched Elements.
Element interface

classes.name: zope.structuredtext.stdom.NodeList

methods.name: zope.structuredtext.stdom.NodeList.item
documentation: Returns the index-th item in the collection

methods.name: zope.structuredtext.stdom.NodeList.getLength
documentation: The length of the NodeList

NodeList interface - Provides the abstraction of an ordered
collection of nodes.

Python extensions: can use sequence-style 'len', 'getitem', and
'for..in' constructs.

classes.name: zope.structuredtext.stdom.NamedNodeMap

methods.name: zope.structuredtext.stdom.NamedNodeMap.item
documentation:
Returns the index-th item in the map.

This is arbitrary because maps have no order.

methods.name: zope.structuredtext.stdom.NamedNodeMap.getLength
documentation: The length of the NodeList

methods.name: zope.structuredtext.stdom.NamedNodeMap.getNamedItem
documentation:
Retrieves a node specified by name. Parameters:
name Name of a node to retrieve. Return Value A Node (of any
type) with the specified name, or None if the specified name
did not identify any node in the map.

NamedNodeMap interface - Is used to represent collections
of nodes that can be accessed by name.  NamedNodeMaps are not
maintained in any particular order.

Python extensions: can use sequence-style 'len', 'getitem', and
'for..in' constructs, and mapping-style 'getitem'.

methods.name: zope.structuredtext.stdom.Attr.getSpecified
documentation:
If this attribute was explicitly given a value in the
original document, this is true; otherwise, it is false.

Attr interface - The Attr interface represents an attriubte in an
Element object. Attr objects inherit the Node Interface

functions.name: zope.structuredtext.stng.indention
documentation: Find the number of leading spaces. If none, return 0.

functions.name: zope.structuredtext.stng.insert
documentation:
Find what will be the parant paragraph of a sentence and return
that paragraph's sub-paragraphs. The new paragraph will be
appended to those sub-paragraphs

functions.name: zope.structuredtext.stng.display
documentation:
Runs through the structure and prints out the paragraphs. If
the insertion works correctly, display's results should mimic the
orignal paragraphs.

functions.name: zope.structuredtext.stng.display2
documentation:
Runs through the structure and prints out the paragraphs. If
the insertion works correctly, display's results should mimic the
orignal paragraphs.

functions.name: zope.structuredtext.stng.findlevel
documentation:
Remove all level information of levels with a greater level of
indentation. Then return which level should insert this paragraph

functions.name: zope.structuredtext.stng.structurize
documentation:
Accepts paragraphs, which is a list of lines to be
parsed. structurize creates a structure which mimics the
structure of the paragraphs.  Structure =>
[paragraph,[sub-paragraphs]]

documentation: UNIX or DOS line endings, respectively

classes.name: zope.structuredtext.stng.StructuredTextTable

methods.name: zope.structuredtext.stng.StructuredTextTable.getColorizableTexts
documentation:
return a tuple where each item is a column/cell's
contents. The tuple, result, will be of this format.
("r1 col1", "r1=col2", "r2 col1", "r2 col2")

methods.name: zope.structuredtext.stng.StructuredTextTable.setColorizableTexts
documentation:
texts is going to a tuple where each item is the
result of being mapped to the colortext function.
Need to insert the results appropriately into the
individual columns/cells

rows is a list of lists containing tuples, which
represent the columns/cells in each rows.
EX
rows = [[('row 1:column1',1)],[('row2:column1',1)]]
a StructuredTextTable holds StructuredTextRows

classes.name: zope.structuredtext.stng.StructuredTextRow

methods.name: zope.structuredtext.stng.StructuredTextRow.__init__
documentation:
row is a list of tuples, where each tuple is
the raw text for a cell/column and the span
of that cell/column.
EX
[('this is column one',1), ('this is column two',1)]
StructuredTextRow holds StructuredTextColumns

classes.name: zope.structuredtext.stng.StructuredTextColumn
documentation:
StructuredTextColumn is a cell/column in a table.
A cell can hold multiple paragraphs. The cell
is either classified as a StructuredTextTableHeader
or StructuredTextTableData.
this holds the text of a table cell

classes.name: zope.structuredtext.document.Document

methods.name: zope.structuredtext.document.Document.parse
documentation:
Parse accepts a raw_string, an expr to test the raw_string,
and the raw_string's subparagraphs.

Parse will continue to search through raw_string until
all instances of expr in raw_string are found.

If no instances of expr are found, raw_string is returned.
Otherwise a list of substrings and instances is returned

methods.name: zope.structuredtext.document.Document.color_text
documentation: Search the paragraph for each special structure

methods.name: zope.structuredtext.document.Document.doc_sgml
documentation: SGML text is ignored and outputed as-is

Class instance calls [ex.=> x()] require a structured text
structure. Doc will then parse each paragraph in the structure
and will find the special structures within each paragraph.
Each special structure will be stored as an instance. Special
structures within another special structure are stored within
the 'top' structure
EX : '-underline this-' => would be turned into an underline
instance. '-underline **this**' would be stored as an underline
instance with a strong instance stored in its string

classes.name: zope.structuredtext.document.DocumentWithImages
documentation: Document with images

methods.name: zope.structuredtext.html.HTML.table
documentation:
A StructuredTextTable holds StructuredTextRow(s) which
holds StructuredTextColumn(s). A StructuredTextColumn
is a type of StructuredTextParagraph and thus holds
the actual data.
